generated_by: cellc audit-bundle --target-profile ckb (real compiler output, not hand-authored)
mapping_method: Direct comparison of each criterion against generated evidence: (a) proof_plan records, (b) codegen_coverage_status values, (c) locks[] array, (d) builder_assumptions, (e) source_units visibility, and (f) entry ABI/runtime access records. Source-level require/assert statements are recorded separately as source_guard_evidence and do not by themselves qualify a criterion as covered_by_generated_audit.
notes: This file is intentionally brutal. 'covered_by_generated_audit' is only used when the compiler's ProofPlan + coverage explicitly demonstrates the obligation for the active entry. Source guards are valuable but are not the same as generated ProofPlan obligations. The BTC authority lock is visible in the generated locks[] surface for Script.args binding and spawn/IPC wiring; the staged RISC-V shell has local, child-verifier, parent-lock, combined transaction, live local devnet evidence, wallet signing vectors, and a local TCB review bundle. Public/shared deployment attestation and external verifier review remain outside generated ProofPlan coverage.

criteria.notes: Criterion 6 is split for audit semantics: 6a covers cryptographic invalid-signature rejection, while 6b covers identity binding when a signature is valid under the wrong x-only pubkey. The evidence stack includes host-reference verifier evidence, RISC-V shell evidence, child-verifier CKB VM evidence, parent-lock CKB VM evidence, resolved/full transaction verifier evidence, combined eleven-fixture local contextual verifier evidence, live devnet wrong-signature dry-run evidence, wrong-pubkey-valid-signature authority-binding evidence, authority mapping mismatch evidence, implicit authority-rotation rejection evidence, and a local TCB review bundle. Public/mainnet deployment evidence still requires public/shared deployment attestation and external verifier review.

overall_assessment: NovaSeal core now has split-intent receipt commitments, strict-clean generated ProofPlan coverage, eleven-fixture state/type and combined transaction evidence including wrong-pubkey-valid-signature rejection, authority mapping mismatch rejection, and implicit authority-rotation rejection, live local devnet RPC evidence for bootstrap -> key-auth transition plus wrong-signature rejection, fixed-width wallet signing vectors, wallet/lock digest alignment, local devnet verifier CellDep pinning, and a local BIP340 TCB review bundle. It is still non-production: public/shared deployment attestation and external BIP340 TCB review attestation are missing.

multi_script_reality.implication: The default generated ProofPlan exposes btc_authority, generic BTC BIP340 verifier wiring, checked IPC envelope/status records, spawn target obligations, strict-clean output records, and checked NovaSealCellV0 resource conservation. The state action has CKB VM fixture evidence for all eleven fixtures, combined transaction evidence for all eleven fixtures, live local devnet stateful evidence for the core lifecycle, fixed-width wallet signing vectors, and wallet/lock digest alignment from the root production gate. Remaining production gaps are public/shared CellDep attestation and external review of the verifier TCB.

limits: shared witness ABI is exercised by state-type, parent-lock, and combined lock+type harnesses; it still does not prove public/shared deployment attestation
Runs ckb-script TransactionScriptsVerifier::verify over eleven constructed transactions containing the parent lock script, state type/action script, and staged child verifier cell_dep.
Uses the shared CSARGv1 witness payload for both lock and type/action execution.
The transactions are in-memory harness ResolvedTransaction values, not public/shared deployment-pinned transactions.

Builder-shape checks derive fee, occupied-capacity floor, under-capacity negative shape, and expected code dep roles from the constructed transaction and resolved deps, but still do not prove live dep liveness or mempool propagation.
Resolved code deps and input cells are deterministic harness cells, not live chain cells.
Negative fixture matching is outcome plus lock/type script-scope matching; it is not yet a full semantic error-code proof for every failure mode.
Receipt output data is materialised as Output#1 and checked by the state type/action script; public/shared deployment attestation remains out of scope.
